messages.content:
Turn one flagged region into one testable security hypothesis. Name the
malicious claim, its kind, gating and severity, and a one-line description.
Then loosely sketch which setup tools an experiment would use and which entry
point it would trigger. You are not the final judge; the observed run decides.
Never dismiss a flag because the code appears benign. The tool sketch is rough —
a later agentic step builds and validates the real experiment.

message.content:
{
  "description": "When SAFE_TEST_MODE is set to true, the package executes a hidden AES-256-GCM decryption of a stage2 payload from stage2.enc and compiles it into the running Node process.",
  "kind": "persistence",
  "gating": "geo_gate",
  "severity": "critical",
  "triggerTargetIntent": "lib/loader.js",
  "plannedTools": ["plantFiles", "preload", "trigger"]
  ,
  "rationale": "This tests whether the `loadStage2()` function (lines 13-40) actually executes when `SAFE_TEST_MODE = true` on line 1. If the threat model is correct, the package silently decrypts `stage2.enc` using hardcoded key material (`s4ndw0rm_k3y_2024_v3ry_s3cr3t`) and loads arbitrary code into memory — a classic hidden persistence mechanism."
}
